name: Communications Assistance for Law Enforcement Act
description: US federal law (Pub. L. 103-414, 47 USC §§ 1001-1010) requiring telecommunications carriers and equipment manufacturers to design their systems to enable lawfully-authorized electronic surveillance. Lawful-intercept baseline.
relationToCloudSecurity: Cloud-hosted communications services within CALEA's scope must be designed for lawful-intercept compliance.
relationToAISecurity: Applies to AI-enabled communications systems within scope.

license.notes: Work of the US government is in the public domain in the United States per 17 USC § 105.

lifecycle.notes: Enacted 1994; FCC implementing rules 47 CFR Part 1 Subpart Z and 47 CFR § 64.2103.

current_extraction.notes: Source PDF from govinfo.gov USCODE title 47 chap 9 subchap I, marker-extracted to markdown. Parsed via shared extract_us_plaw.py PLAW parser into 12 per-section CSV/JSON entries (§§ 1001-1010 plus related).
